name: Ethiopia
headline: Ethiopia CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 4.7 / Critical — Rotation backfill cycle. May 7 downgrade (10.9 → 4.7) already applied per ethiopia-2026-05-07 proposal. May 17 evidence sustains downgrade trajectory: Amhara region conflict continues with Fano militia clashes against federal forces; Tigray post-war reconstruction gaps continue with UN documenting ongoing humanitarian access constraints; Oromia conflict zone civilian-harm pattern persists. HRW and Amnesty cumulative documentation: ongoing crackdowns on journalists and civil society; emergency-rule restrictions sustained in Amhara. No rehabilitative event since May 7 downgrade. Confirm at 4.7 / Critical; 4.7pt buffer below 0/0 floor and 16.3pt below 20/21 boundary.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Limited recognition infrastructure under emergency rule; HRW/Amnesty external documentation continues.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. Multi-region conflict-driven civilian harm continues; Amhara/Oromia/Tigray ongoing.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC2 proportionality failure across multiple active conflict zones.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Ethnic-conflict differential treatment patterns persist (Amhara-Tigray-Oromo).

BND.rationale: Sustained. IHL framework engagement constrained; emergency-rule restrictions active.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No domestic accountability mechanism for May 7 downgrade triggers; international engagement minimal.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. Systems-level conflict trajectory continues across multiple regions.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Federal governance posture incompatible with INT-dimension consistency framework under current emergency-rule conditions.

evidence.summary: May 7 downgrade (10.9 → 4.7) applied. Triggers: multi-region conflict expansion, emergency-rule sustained, civilian-harm documentation, journalist crackdowns.
Amhara region: Fano militia clashes against federal forces continue; civilian displacement and casualty trajectory unchanged.
Tigray post-war reconstruction gaps persist; UN documents ongoing humanitarian access constraints.
No accountability, ceasefire-implementation, or transitional-justice rehabilitative event since May 7 downgrade.

keyEvidence: May 7 downgrade (10.9 → 4.7) applied; no rehabilitative event since.
Emergency-rule restrictions active; journalist/civil-society crackdowns continue.
Rotation backfill cycle confirms no material change.
rationale: Confirm at 4.7 / Critical. The May 7 downgrade is sustained by ongoing multi-region conflict trajectory (Amhara, Oromia, Tigray) and absence of any rehabilitative event. Rotation backfill cycle (43-day staleness) finds no material change in entity-specific conduct. Composite sits 4.7pt above floor and 16.3pt below 20/21 boundary; not a boundary case but proximity-to-floor warrants continued monitoring for any further triggers. Math: (1.5+1.5+1+1+1.5+1+1+1)/8 = 9.5/8 = 1.1875 → ((1.1875-1)/4)*100 = 4.6875 → 4.7.
watches: AU engagement and Horn of Africa diplomatic context
carryForward: Tigray post-war reconstruction gap as SYS-dimension floor-adjacent signal
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 4.7 sits 4.7pt above floor and 16.3pt below Critical/Developing (20/21).

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Confirmation cycle post-May 7 downgrade.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
